growth_records.strain: AA (adaptive-laboratory-evolution mutant selected for growth on adipic acid; DSM wild-type derivative)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM), adapted from Hartmans et al. 1989
growth_records.cite: Op de Hipt, Leonie et al. Evolutionary engineering of Geobacillus thermoleovorans for growth on adipic acid and 1,4-butanediol. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2026). DOI:10.1007/s00253-026-13836-8. [PMC13135583]
growth_records.snippet: The two selected evolved strains, denoted G. thermoleovorans AA and G. thermoleovorans BDO, grew with a rate of 0.054 ± 0.004 h−1 on AA (Fig. 1C)... whereas no decrease in AA below 15 mM was observed
growth_records.notes: Reported mu = 0.054 ± 0.004 h-1 (batch, sole C-source 30 mM AA). Doubling time computed td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.054 = 12.84 h (calculated). MSM phosphate buffer for monomers was 'threefold' the glucose-medium buffer (22.3 mM K2HPO4 + 13.6 mM NaH2PO4), i.e. ~66.9 mM K2HPO4 + ~40.8 mM NaH2PO4 (derived from '20 mM glucose ... 22.3 mM K2HPO4 and 13.6 mM NaH2PO4 or the PBAT monomers ... a threefold' buffer). Full trace-element/salt composition is inherited from Hartmans et al. 1989 and not enumerated in-text. Growth ceased with ~15 mM AA remaining (transporter Km limitation). Abstract reports 'up to 0.10 h-1 on AA' achieved only after ADDITIONAL ALE on reduced (15 mM) AA; that value has no error/strain-specific figure in-text so recorded separately in notes only. No quantitative substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates (mmol/gDW/h) reported. pH set/controlled at 7 in PBAT bioreactor runs; batch pH not actively controlled.

growth_records.strain: BDO (adaptive-laboratory-evolution mutant selected for growth on 1,4-butanediol)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM), adapted from Hartmans et al. 1989
growth_records.cite: Op de Hipt, Leonie et al. Evolutionary engineering of Geobacillus thermoleovorans for growth on adipic acid and 1,4-butanediol. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2026). DOI:10.1007/s00253-026-13836-8. [PMC13135583]
growth_records.snippet: grew with a rate of ... 0.13 ± 0.010 h−1 on BDO (Fig. 1F), respectively. Complete metabolization of BDO by the evolved strain was achieved
growth_records.notes: Reported mu = 0.13 ± 0.010 h-1 (batch, sole C-source 45 mM BDO). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.13 = 5.33 h (calculated). Complete BDO consumption reported (qualitative), no quantitative uptake/secretion rate given. MSM buffer for monomers = threefold glucose-medium buffer (see AA record). Temperature 60 C, 200 rpm.

growth_records.cite: Op de Hipt, Leonie et al. Evolutionary engineering of Geobacillus thermoleovorans for growth on adipic acid and 1,4-butanediol. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2026). DOI:10.1007/s00253-026-13836-8. [PMC13135583]
growth_records.snippet: The dilution rates were 0.15 h−1 for G. thermoleovorans BDO and 0.04 h−1 for G. thermoleovorans AA... chemostat cultivations were carried out (Fig. S1)
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.15 h-1 (calculated identity at steady state). Doubling time td = ln2/0.15 = 4.62 h. pH monitored but not actively controlled (value not stated). No quantitative uptake/secretion rates reported. Carbon-source concentration in feed not stated in main text.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) with adipic acid
growth_records.cite: Op de Hipt, Leonie et al. Evolutionary engineering of Geobacillus thermoleovorans for growth on adipic acid and 1,4-butanediol. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2026). DOI:10.1007/s00253-026-13836-8. [PMC13135583]
growth_records.snippet: The dilution rates were 0.15 h−1 for G. thermoleovorans BDO and 0.04 h−1 for G. thermoleovorans AA.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.04 h-1 (calculated identity). Doubling time td = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. pH not stated. No quantitative uptake/secretion rates.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) with 20 mM glucose
growth_records.cite: Op de Hipt, Leonie et al. Evolutionary engineering of Geobacillus thermoleovorans for growth on adipic acid and 1,4-butanediol. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2026). DOI:10.1007/s00253-026-13836-8. [PMC13135583]
growth_records.snippet: the unevolved strain was also cultivated on glucose at both dilution rates.
growth_records.notes: Unevolved wild type in glucose-limited chemostat at D = 0.15 h-1 -> mu = 0.15 h-1 (steady-state identity). td = ln2/0.15 = 4.62 h. Glucose MSM: 20 mM glucose, 22.3 mM K2HPO4, 13.6 mM NaH2PO4 (stated). No uptake/secretion rates.
